title: QuorumEvaluator — case matrix
description: Exhaustive test cases for QuorumEvaluator::evaluate(requirement). Consumed by the module's integration tests + documentation. Every case names the input state + expected transition. QuorumEvaluator is called on every ApprovalDecisionRecorded event to decide whether the requirement should transition (pending → satisfied | failed) + whether the parent instance should transition.

quorum_type_matrix.description: One approve suffices. One reject fails IF no more approvers can still approve (queue drained).

cases.notes: 1 approve → immediate satisfied. u2 + u3 don't need to decide.
Only u3 left. Still could approve — stay pending.
All three rejected. No one left to approve.

quorum_type_matrix.description: Every resolved approver must approve. One reject fails immediately.

cases.notes: u3 still hasn't decided. Stay pending.
u2's reject blocks unanimous approve. Fail immediately (don't wait for u3).

quorum_type_matrix.description: At least quorum_n approves; reject fails IF the remaining approver count can't reach quorum_n.

cases.notes: 3 rejects. Only u4 + u5 left (max 2 more approves) — cannot reach quorum_n=3.
2 approves + 1 reject. u4 + u5 still can push to 4 approves ≥ quorum_n=3.
2 approves + 2 rejects. u5 remaining — could push approves to 3, hits quorum_n=3.
2 approves + 3 rejects. All decisions in. approves=2 < quorum_n=3 → failed.

instance_transitions.description: All requirements satisfied → instance transitions to approved.
One requirement failed → instance transitions to rejected (regardless of other requirements' status).
One requirement pending + others satisfied → instance stays pending.
Sequential ordering — group at sort_order=0 satisfies → group at sort_order=1 unblocks.
expected_events: NotifyApproversJob dispatches ApprovalRequestedNotification to apr_B's resolved_approvers

notes: 'Cannot reach quorum_n' is a projection — QuorumEvaluator checks IF (approve_count + remaining_approvers_count) < quorum_n → fail immediately even before every approver decides.
Blocked requirements don't accept decisions. If a caller POSTs approve on a blocked requirement, the observer refuses with REQUIREMENT_NOT_PENDING.
Withdraw / cancel transitions ALL pending + blocked requirements to failed (short-circuits the whole flow).

The reconciler recomputes quorum on delegation-drift — if a delegation activates mid-flow, the delegate is ADDED to the pending requirement's resolved_approvers (via supplementary reminders — not by retroactive substitution).
